---
title: "Bertin 1967 — Barres empilées (3 premiers départements)"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r package_setup, include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(here)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 7,
  fig.height = 5
)

sector_colors <- c(Agriculture = "#4daf4a", Industry = "#ff7f00", Services = "#377eb8")

read_csv_locale <- function(path, ...) {
  readr::read_csv(
    path,
    locale = readr::locale(decimal_mark = ".", grouping_mark = ","),
    ...
  )
}
```

```{r load_data}
bertin_1 <- read_csv_locale(here::here("course", "bertin-1967.csv")) |>
  slice(1:1) |>
  arrange(desc(agriculture)) |>
  mutate(department = factor(department, levels = department)) |>
  pivot_longer(c(agriculture, industry, services),
               names_to = "sector", values_to = "pct") |>
  mutate(
    sector = recode(sector,
                    agriculture = "Agriculture",
                    industry = "Industry",
                    services = "Services"
    ),
    sector = factor(sector, levels = c("Services", "Industry", "Agriculture"))
  )
```

```{r stacked_bar}
ggplot(
  bertin_1,
  aes(
    x = department,
    y = pct,
    fill = sector
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sector_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Composition de la population active ",
    subtitle = "Département de l'Ain",
    x = NULL,
    y = "%",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```
```{r pie_chart}
ggplot(
  bertin_1,
  aes(
    x = department,
    y = pct,
    fill = sector
  )
) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_polar("y", start = 0) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = sector_colors) +
  labs(
    title = "Composition de la population active",
    subtitle = "Département de l'Ain",
    x = NULL,
    y = "%",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```
